article_title: Update to packaged sales from a manufacturer on-site store
body: These changes apply to the following licences: Manufacturers with anon-site store endorsementare now permitted to sell packaged liquor almost anywhere on the manufacturing site. However, packaged liquor sales must still be attributed to the on-site store endorsement no matter where they occur. Packaged liquor sales are not permitted in picnic consumption-only areas or in co-located businesses, other than a food-primary or liquor-primary establishment that is held by the same licensee. Drive-through type sales remain prohibited, including sales in or next to driveways, thoroughfares, roadways, and parking lots. Packaged liquor sold from the on-site store cannot be consumed at the manufacturer site except in apicnic area. Packaged liquor sales from an off-site storage facility remain prohibited, but delivery fromoff-site storage is temporarily permitted. To read all the rules for these changes, please refer to the: An example of a mobile point of sale would be a patron purchasing a product while on a guided tour. The product could be available for delivery or pick-up from the store at the patron’s convenience. Manufacturers with an on-site store endorsement don’t need to apply for this change to take effect. Bulletins are for general information and may not apply to all situations. Bulletins do not constitute legal advice nor are they a comprehensive statement of the legal obligations that arise under the Liquor Control and Licensing Act, Cannabis Control and Licensing Act, regulations, or any other applicable laws. When interpreting and applying the information contained in the Bulletins, you are encouraged to seek advice from your professional advisors as appropriate in the circumstances. The B.C. Public Service acknowledges the territories of First Nations around B.C. and is grateful to carry out our work on these lands. We acknowledge the rights, interests, priorities, and concerns of all Indigenous Peoples - First Nations, Métis, and Inuit - respecting and acknowledging their distinct cultures, histories, rights, laws, and governments.

minutes_briefs.title: BC liquor manufacturers can now sell packaged liquor anywhere on-site
minutes_briefs.summary: BC liquor manufacturers with an on-site store endorsement can now sell packaged liquor almost anywhere on their manufacturing site, not just inside the store. However, sales must still be recorded under the on-site store endorsement. Sales are not allowed in picnic-only areas, co-located businesses (except food-primary or liquor-primary businesses held by the same licensee), or through drive-throughs. Packaged liquor bought on-site cannot be consumed there except in a picnic area. Off-site storage sales are still banned, but delivery from off-site storage is temporarily allowed. No application is needed for this change.
